titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 32

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=114 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:00.327985+00 | translation moa=115 status=live modified=2016-06-07 11:08:39.630801+00

p.cl: Omnibus presentes litteras inspecturis Magister Guillelmus Parmensis domini pape Capellanus ec eiusdem contradictarum auditor Salutem in domino .

cum Radulfus de helig’ clericus procurator amissus de speciali domini pape . mandato . pro . . preposito et Capitulo ecclesie sancte Marie sancti ndree in Scotia impetrasset litteras apostolicas sub hac forma . Innocentius episcopus etc. Dilectis filiis . . Preposito ⁊ Capitulo ecclesie sancte Marie Sancti Andree in Scotia Salutem etc. Cum a nobis petitur quod iustum est ⁊ honestum tam uigor equitatis quam ordo exigit rationis . ut id per sollicitudinem officij nostri ad debitum perducatur effectum . Eapropter dilecti in domino filij uestris iustis postulationibus grato concurrentes assensu ecclesias . possessiones ⁊ alia bona uestra que in presentiarum rationabiliter possidetis aut in futurum iustis modis prestante domino poteritis adipisci sub beati petri ⁊ nostra protectione suscipimus ⁊ ea uobis ⁊ ecclesie uestre auctoritate apostolica confirmamus ⁊ presentis scripti patrocinio communimus . Nulli ergo etc. Si quis autem etc. Datum lugdun’ xii kaldendas . Septembris pontificatus nostri anno Septimo . Eisdem litteris petrus de Vercell’ procurator prioris ⁊ Capituli sancti andree cathedralis ecclesie in Scotia . pro ipsis in audientia publica contradixit quas tandem ea conditione absoluit quo per predictas litteras nullum priuillegiis . predictorum prioris ⁊ Capituli sancti andree ecclesie cathedralis prejudicium generetur nec ad iurisdictionem executorum eorundem priuilegiorum impediendam aliquatenus intendatur . Quod pars coram nobis . In Cujus rei testimonium presentes litteras fieri fecimus ⁊ nostro muniri sigillo . Datum lugduni .ij. idus Septembris pontificatus domini Innocentii pape iiij anno Septimo .

div.p: To all who shall view the present letters, Master William of Parma, Chaplain of the Lord Pope and auditor contradictarum of the same, Greeting in the Lord: You should know that when Ralph de Helig’, clerk, procurator admitted by special mandate of the the lord pope, on behalf of the provost and chapter of the church of St Mary, of St Andrews in Scotia, petitioned for apostolic letters under this form: Innocent the bishop, etc., To his beloved sons, the Provost and Chapter of the church of St Mary, of St Andrews in Scotia, Greeting etc. When what is requested from us is just and worthy, the force of equity as well as the order of reason require that it should be carried through to its due effect by the consideration of our office. Therefore, beloved sons in the Lord, we gladly approve and consent to your just requests, and we take under the protection of St Peter and ourselves the churches, possessions, and your other goods which you reasonbly possess now or which by just means and God’s gift you will be able to acquire in the future, and we confirm them to you and your church by apostolic authority and defend them by the protection of the present document. Therefore let no one etc. If, however, anyone etc. Given at Lyons on 12 Kalends of September [21 August], in the seventh year of our pontificate. Peter de Vercelli, procurator of the prior and chapter of the cathedral church of St Andrews in Scotia, objected to the same letter on their behalf in public audience, but in the end withdrew on the condition that any prejudice should not be generated by the aforesaid letters to the privileges of the aforesaid prior and chapter of the catehdral church of St Andrews, nor be used in any way to impede the jusrisdiction of the executors of the same priviliges, which the other party firmly promised in our sight. In testimony of this matter we have caused the present letters to be made and protected by our seal. Given at Lyons on the 2 Ides of of September [12 September] in the seventh year of the pontificate of the lord pope, Innocent IV.
